The '''Epsom Asteroid Belt'''<ref name="LimpingLady" /> was located in the [[Epsom system/Legends|Epsom system]], a part of the [[Quiberon sector/Legends|Quiberon sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA Epsom" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Saurian (species)|Saurian]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Sithless Leethe]] owned a [[Sithless Leethe's summer residence|summer residence]] in the [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid belt]].<ref name="LimpingLady" />

{{RPG|"[[Limping Lady (article)|Limping Lady]]"}}
At one point, a group of individuals on<ref name="LimpingLady" /> the [[Arkanis sector/Legends|Arkanis sector]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] purchased the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] ''[[Limping Lady]]'' from Leethe. The Saurian accepted a fraction of the total asking price—25,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]—as a down payment while the difference was to be paid in full, either on Tatooine or at Leethe's [[Summer/Legends|summer]] residence in the Epsom Asteroid Belt, within one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] of the sale.<ref name="LimpingLady" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
The Epsom Asteroid Belt was mentioned in "[[Limping Lady (article)|Limping Lady]]," a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure written by [[Peter M. Schweighofer]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and published in the [[Challenge 64|sixty-fourth issue]] of the [[Challenge (magazine)|''Challenge'']] magazine in [[September]] [[1992]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="LimpingLady" /> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> This article assumes the scenario plays out as described.<ref name="LimpingLady" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Epsom [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the Epsom Asteroid Belt, in grid square S-15.<ref name="TEA Epsom" />
